title: Video by musicallyproper
description:
In 1982, Conrad Schnitzler—once a member of Tangerine Dream and Kluster—dropped ‘The Shark Eats Ice.’ A track so icy, mechanical, and satirical it feels like electroclash 20 years early.

Schnitzler came from Berlin’s art-school avant-garde, where Dada thrived on absurd, vivid phrases that stripped words of rational meaning. 

Born during WWI, Dada was an anti-art movement that embraced nonsense and provocation as a way to reject logic and convention. 

‘The Shark Eats Ice’ feels like exactly that—a machine-beat predator devouring something cold and empty, a surreal title that mirrors the track’s satire of consumer electronics and the dehumanizing chill of machine culture.

(Visuals by the synth performance art pioneers, Tik and Tok)

#conradschnitzler #electro #electroclash #synthmusic #mimes

title: Video by jake_whan
description:
You don’t need to spend $25,000 on stem cells. You can do it for free, using your own body and breath. 

Isn’t it profound that our own body can heal itself!? Well… the generations that came before us knew this before we were controlled and manipulated to believe that we couldn’t. And our own breath is one of the greatest tools to allow our body’s to heal. 

Intermittent hypoxia is something I’ve been doing for 4+ years now. It has helped me clear my sinuses, overcome injuries and gain a better quality of life and well-being. 

Give this practice a try and I teach a magnitude of breathwork practices inside my program Ascension University. Comment “ASCENSION” and I’ll send you the information and link to join. 🙏

title: Post by uncover.quantum
description:
🌌 A RUSSIAN QUANTUM PHYSICIST DISCOVERED HOW TO SLIDE BETWEEN PARALLEL REALITIES 🌌
This is genuinely mind-bending. In 1993, Vadim Zeland quit his job after a profound insight: reality exists as infinite parallel tracks, like movie versions already filmed. And we can “slide” between them by changing our inner state.
His counter-intuitive discovery:
The harder you want something, the further it gets pushed away. Treating goals like “no big deal” makes them manifest faster than desperately wanting them ever could.
Why this happens:
When you assign high importance to something, you create “excess potential” - energetic resistance that the universe balances by creating obstacles. The more you push, the more resistance you generate.
His 3-step no-big-deal method:
Step 1: Lower the importance
Say “It would be nice if this happened, but it’s no big deal if it doesn’t.” Feel the relief when you release the pressure.
Step 2: Act as if it’s already arranged
Move through your day assuming the universe has handled it. Take inspired action, but don’t force.
Step 3: Slide between realities
When obstacles appear, imagine stepping sideways into a parallel reality where they don’t exist.
Why this works:
Your emotional state broadcasts a frequency attracting matching circumstances. Desperation attracts more desperation. Calm confidence attracts opportunities.
The no-big-deal state is the highest manifestation frequency because it contains zero resistance.
What to watch for:
Synchronicities increase. Doors open easily. Helpful people appear. Obstacles dissolve. These are signs you’re sliding to better timelines.
The truth:
What you chase runs away. What you allow comes closer. Your desires exist in parallel realities right now - you just need to slide into the right track by changing your inner state.

Follow @uncover.quantum for reality-bending insights that challenge everything

#RealityTransurfing #QuantumPhysics #Manifestation #ParallelRealities #VadimZeland #ConsciousnessScience #QuantumMechanics #RealityShifting #MindScience​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

title: Video by artart.en
description:
🎬 Akira and Hiroko (2008) – Directed by Michel Gondry, from the anthology film <Tokyo!>

Tokyo! brings together Michel Gondry, Leos Carax, and Bong Joon-ho — three visionary directors capturing the city through their own cinematic lenses. The featured short, Akira and Hiroko (Interior Design), marks the opening segment directed by Gondry.

Hiroko follows her boyfriend, an aspiring filmmaker, from Hokkaido to Tokyo. Feeling unseen and wondering, “Why am I here?”, she drifts through the city’s indifference — until one day, her body begins to change in the strangest way.

title: Video by jeetinsight
description:
Lanthimos refused to explain the ending, saying mystery was essential.

Dogtooth (2009), directed by Yorgos Lanthimos, is one of the most unsettling and unique films of modern European cinema. The story explores extreme family control, isolation, and the ways people can manipulate reality.

The film’s intense scenes were filmed with practical effects, minimal music, and precise acting choices to maintain tension and unease. Actors were instructed to keep emotional reactions minimal, adding to the eerie, controlled atmosphere. Critics call it one of the most controversial and talked-about films in recent years, praised for its bold storytelling, strange world-building, and unforgettable visual style. 
.
.
.
#dogtooth #yorgoslanthimos #lanthimos #psychologicalthriller #darkcinema #cinephile #movielovers #cinemalover #europeancinema #jeetinsight

title: Video by expansionwithdrjess
description:
Most people think manifestation is about what you want.

It’s not. It’s about who your nervous system thinks you are.

And that identity was formed long before you ever chose it. When you were little, your brain lived in a highly programmable state.

You absorbed tone, chaos, pressure, roles, expectations…
and your body built an identity around whatever felt safest.
The strong one.
The invisible one.
The caretaker.
The perfectionist.
The one who doesn’t need anything.
The one who earns love by overfunctioning.
That isn’t personality. That’s survival coding.

And here’s the kicker:
Your nervous system will recreate ANY reality that matches that identity.

Not because you’re sabotaging yourself,
but because your body is loyal to whatever once kept you alive.

If you want a new reality, you don’t need more affirmations.

You need a new identity.

👇 Tell me in the comments:
Which survival identity do YOU resonate with?
The strong one, the pleaser, the perfectionist, the invisible one, or something else entirely?

✨ Follow for Part 3: Why Your Nervous System Doesn’t Believe Your Affirmations.

title: Video by celebratethelittlewins
description:
🚨 Comment RELIEF and I’ll DM you a free copy of my relief guide or get it at the link in my bio

*This is not medical advice, I’m just sharing my story. Always consult with your healthcare professional regarding your health. 

➡️ Save this video for later
➡️ Share with a friend
➡️ Post to your stories 

Have you heard of this silent epidemic? sharing my story because if someone would have shared this information with me a few years ago, I could have avoided being in a wheelchair and having to learn how to walk again.

these antibiotics are often handed out like candy with no regard for how serious side effects can be. the short list is they can attack your central nervous system, gut, brain, heart, tendons/nerves, eyes and kidneys. they cause mitochondrial damage and the FDA has said (for YEARS) that they should be reserved for life and death situations. often times doctors are completely unaware of these debilitating side effects as they’re commonly prescribed for all sorts of minor issues like UTIs and ear infections. 

The main FQ Antibiotics to educate yourself on before it’s too late: 
ciprofloxacin (Cipro)
gemifloxacin (Factive)
levofloxacin (Levaquin)
moxifloxacin (Avelox)
ofloxacin (Floxin)

➡️ FQ eyedrops and ear drops also can flox you 😮‍💨 these are also given out to pets. 💀

FQs can also have a cumulative effect and even if you’ve done “fine” with it in the past, it’s like playing russian roulette — you can have a reaction years later and “the bomb” can go off with sometimes only one pill. 

this time 3 years ago i was suffering with 70+ 😳😳😳 unexplainable symptoms and had to connect the dots on my own. I wasn’t sure if i would survive it. today i have less than 5 symptoms. 😭❤️‍🩹 

if you have had a reaction to a fluoroquinolone and have been left to your own suffering, feel free to DM me or drop a comment - i dont give medical advice but I’m happy to share what has been helping me heal. 🫶🏼 

❤️‍🩹repost to your story, send this to someone you love or tag them in the comments — you may just save their life. 

Press / podcasts / blogs: please get in touch at hello@celebratethelittlewins.com to help me spread the word 🦋

title: Video by movies_times_12
description:
Caligula (1979) is one of the most controversial films ever made, blending historical drama with shocking explicit content. Inspired by the real-life Roman emperor Caligula, the movie follows his rise to power and his descent into madness, cruelty, and extreme obsession.

The film depicts ancient Rome as a place of absolute power, corruption, violence, and unchecked desire. Caligula’s rule is shown as brutal and chaotic, where human lives, loyalty, and morals mean nothing. As emperor, he indulges in sadistic pleasures, manipulation, and public humiliation, slowly destroying everyone around him—including himself.

What makes Caligula infamous is not just its story, but its explicit scenes, which caused massive controversy worldwide. Many versions of the film were banned, censored, or edited in several countries. Despite this, the movie gained cult status for its bold themes, disturbing imagery, and portrayal of how power without limits can turn a man into a monster.

Caligula is not an easy watch. It’s disturbing, uncomfortable, and extreme—but for many, it remains a symbol of cinema without boundaries, showing the darkest side of human desire and authority.
. 
#Caligula1979 #ControversialCinema #CultFilm #RomanEmpire #DisturbingMovies #BannedMovies #DarkCinema #HistoricalDrama #ExtremeCinema #CultClassic

title: Video by cinesospeso
description:
Osgood Perkins has officially cemented his status as the architect of modern atmospheric horror with Keeper (2025). Collaborating again with cinematographer Jeremy Cox, Perkins utilizes suffocating negative space and deliberate, voyeuristic framing to transform a simple cabin retreat into a claustrophobic nightmare. The terror here is purely architectural; the camera lingers on empty corners and open doorways, forcing the audience to actively scan the frame for threats that may not even exist.
Tatiana Maslany delivers a career-defining performance of unraveling sanity, grounding the folk-horror elements in a terrifyingly tangible emotional reality. While the screenplay by Nick Lepard builds a foundation of relationship toxicity, it is Perkins’ precise control over tone and sound design—specifically Edo Van Breemen’s subliminal score—that instills a physical sense of dread.This film proves that true horror lies not in the monster, but in the silence before it appears.

Follow @cinesospeso for cinema analysis.
.
.

#horrormovies #horrorfan #creepy

title: Video by explaining
description:
Follow (us) @Explaining to Learn something NEW every day 🧠💫 

Andy Serkis transformed movie history with his performance as Gollum in The Lord of the Rings. 🧙‍♂️🎭

Instead of just lending his voice, Serkis performed the character physically on set using motion capture, crawling, twisting, and acting alongside the cast so animators could capture every movement and emotion. His split personality, switching between Gollum and Sméagol, brought depth, tragedy, and realism to a fully digital character. 🎥✨

The role proved that motion capture could be true acting, not just animation, and it changed how films approach digital characters forever.

Let us know your thoughts below!

•

#explore #storytelling #interesting #reels #learn

title: Post by sdcaw_spirituality
description:
In 1985, Dr. Bruce Lipton found something unsettling: your cells are not responding to reality—they’re responding to what you think reality is. 

He called it the Perception Code. 🧠✨

Bruce Lipton was a cell biologist. 

He studied stem cells—same DNA, different environments, different outcomes. 

One dish created bone. 

One created muscle. 

One created fat. Genes weren’t in control—the environment was. 

And what controlled the environment? 

Your perception. Your beliefs. 

Your cells respond to the world you perceive, not the world that exists. 🌀

Fear changes cell chemistry. 

Stress weakens immunity. 

Hope boosts regeneration. 

Thoughts aren’t mental—they’re molecular. 

Your perception writes instructions on your DNA. 

Most people live in a stress-based perception installed during childhood, keeping the body in survival mode. Healing, growth, creativity shut down. 💫

The 3-step method: Interrupt the fear signal. 

Install a new perception (“What if this works for me?”). 

Feel the future state—emotion flips the genetic switch. 

Rewrite perception → rewrite chemistry → rewrite reality. 🔑

Comment “BLUEPRINT” for the complete system.

.

.
Follow @sdcaw_spirituality for more interesting and thought provoking content

title: Post by thequantumbrief
description:
In 1977, inventor Itzhak Bentov met with intelligence officials to share a radical idea: The universe is a hologram within human awareness.

The CIA later produced a classified 29-page report titled Analysis and Assessment of Gateway Process. It concluded that human consciousness could move beyond physical space and time.

The Missing Page 25 Kept classified for decades, this page eventually revealed statements like “Time is a construct of the conscious mind” and that “There is no past or future, only NOW.” This linked meditation, quantum theory, and brainwave synchronization into a practical framework.

The Method Bentov’s approach involved entering the theta state, synchronizing both brain hemispheres using binaural beats, and applying focused intention to reshape memory and influence probability.

The Tragic End Bentov died in the 1979 Flight 191 crash while traveling to present his findings. He left behind a powerful legacy and one core message:

“You are not a body living in the universe. You are the universe experiencing itself through a body.”

title: Video by therealnurtle
description:
In 1949, Indian mathematician D. R. Kaprekar described a numerical process that revealed a fixed result for four digit numbers in base ten. That result is 6174, now known as Kaprekar’s Constant.

The process works as follows. Take any four digit number with at least two different digits. Rearrange its digits to form the largest possible number and the smallest possible number, then subtract the smaller from the larger. Repeat the same steps with each new result.

No matter which valid four digit number you start with, the sequence reaches 6174 in a finite number of steps. Once reached, the process becomes stable because rearranging 6174 gives 7641 and 1467, and their difference is 6174 again.

This behavior is specific to four digit numbers in the base ten number system. Other digit lengths can have similar constants, but 6174 is unique to four digit decimal numbers and always acts as the final fixed point of the process.

——————
#viral #math #science #news #Nurtle

title: Video by judgysociety
description:
🧮

Kaprekar's constant is the number 6174, a four-digit number that emerges when you repeatedly apply a specific process: take any four-digit number (with at least two different digits), rearrange its digits to form the largest and smallest possible numbers, and subtract the smaller from the larger, always ensuring four digits (using leading zeros if needed). This "Kaprekar's routine" always converges to 6174 within seven iterations, after which the process yields 6174 indefinitely (e.g., 7641 - 1467 = 6174).

title: Video by buelistic
description:
You are not living in an objective world.
You are living inside your nervous system- regulated or unregulated

Neuroscience studies ave discovered that over 90% of sensory data is filtered out before it ever reaches your conscious awareness. What you call “reality” is just your brain predicting what matters most for its safety &survival(this defense is built from past experiences, emotional memories, and expectation)
Like I said, Two people can be in the same room, hear the same words, experience the same event… and process entirely different worlds.

Quantum physics shows us why this matters.
At the subatomic level, particles do not exist as fixed objects. They exist as probabilities of existence. They exist as potential waves/frequencies until measured. Observation-aka your focus-isn’t passive. It forces reality to select. Then It collapses all those different possibilities into one form.

⚡️Again! Remember Your thoughts are electrical signals traveling through neural networks.
🧲Your emotions are magnetic fields generated by the heart & body.Together, they form a signal and it trains what your brain notices, what your body prepares for, and as a result trains the world to show which probabilities to bring to the surface of your experience.
This is why repetition works.
This is why visualization works.
This is why belief👉🏽when paired with emotional certainty👈🏽changes outcomes. Not because you’re “pretending.”But because the nervous system cannot distinguish between an experience vividly imagined and one physically lived. The body calibrates first. Reality follows.

“Reality is merely an illusion, albeit a very persistent one.”
-AAlbert Einstein
The Quanta 2.0 breaks this down precisely, what the world is, how to regulate your state while experiencing it, building coherence, and intentionally raising the probability of the life you want to experience. And once you understand the system, you stop asking for permission, you start directing outcomes.

title: Video by antiagingmission
description:
This One Breathing Technique Increases Brain Blood Flow by 500%

Yes — 5x more blood to your brain and heart just by how you breathe.

Here’s how it works:
1. Take 30 deep, rhythmic breaths — in through the nose, out through the mouth.
2. After the final exhale, hold your breath for as long as you can (aim for ~90 seconds).
3. During this breath-hold, blood flow to the brain and heart increases up to fivefold.

title: Video by film.paranoia
description:
A Page of Madness (1926), directed by Teinosuke Kinugasa, is a landmark of Japanese experimental cinema and one of the most radical silent films ever made. Set almost entirely inside a mental asylum, the film follows a former sailor who takes a job as a janitor in order to stay close to his wife, a patient institutionalized after a psychological breakdown. Rather than telling its story through conventional narrative, the film immerses the viewer in a fractured, subjective world that mirrors the inner lives of its characters.

#JapaneseCinema #FilmHistory #SilentFilm
#Cinephile#APageOfMadness #ExperimentalFilm #AvantGardeCinema #CultCinema#FilmReel #ArthouseFilm #WorldCinema

title: Video by sheturnedsavage
description:
No one talks about this part.

When pain has been your shadow for years,
it stops feeling like something you carry —
and starts feeling like who you are.

You’ve built yourself around it.
Learned how to function with it.
Learned how to explain yourself through it.

The sadness became your language.
The anxiety became your rhythm.
The grief became your identity.

So when someone says, “You’re healing,”
instead of relief… there’s fear.

Who am I without the ache?
Who am I without the story that shaped me?
Who am I without the battles that carved themselves into my bones?

Healing sounds beautiful in theory.
But in reality?
It feels like walking into a quiet room after years of chaos.

And the quiet is unfamiliar.
The quiet is uncomfortable.
The quiet asks you to meet yourself without the armor.

You are not strange for being scared of that.
You are not weak for hesitating at the edge of peace.

When pain has been your compass,
letting it go can feel like losing direction.

But here’s the truth:

You are not your suffering.
You are not the worst thing that happened to you.
You are not the grief that shaped you.

You are the person who survived it.

And the version of you that exists beyond the pain?
She isn’t empty.
She isn’t lost.
She isn’t unrecognisable.

She’s softer.
Stronger.
Quieter in the best way.

It’s okay to be scared of meeting her.

Just don’t run from her.

🕊️ Follow @sheturnedsavage for grief quotes that hold your heart while you learn who you are outside the hurt.

💬 If you’re struggling with your mental health and navigating healing feels overwhelming,
comment or DM “workbook.”
You don’t have to figure this out alone.

#HealingIsScary
#GriefJourney
#IdentityAfterLoss

title: Video by crime.short
description:
In 1836, a man named Leopold von Sacher-Masoch was born in the Austrian Empire.
He was not a criminal, nor a fictional character, but a real writer and thinker whose life would later influence psychology itself.
Masoch grew up in a strict and authoritarian environment.
From a young age, he was deeply affected by ideas of power, punishment, and obedience.
Rather than acting on these thoughts, he explored them through literature.
In 1870, he published his most famous novel, Venus in Furs.
The book was not written to glorify pain, but to examine the psychological conflict between control and submission.
It portrayed a man who willingly gives up power, believing that surrender gives his life meaning.
Years later, European psychiatrists began classifying human behaviors scientifically.
In 1886, Austrian psychiatrist Richard von Krafft-Ebing introduced the term “Masochism” in his medical work, naming it after Sacher-Masoch.
This decision was not made with Masoch’s consent.
When he discovered that his name had become a medical term, he was deeply upset and tried to stop it—but failed.
Leopold von Sacher-Masoch died in 1895, poor and largely forgotten.
Yet his name lived on, permanently attached to a psychological concept he never intended to represent.
Masochism was not born from myth or crime,
but from a real man, whose attempt to understand the human mind ended up defining it in an unexpected way.

title: Post by realratedred
description:
A fan recently went viral after listing her used contact lenses for sale, claiming they had “seen Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour,” with an asking price of $10,000. The bizarre listing quickly sparked reactions across social media, with many questioning how something so personal could be treated like valuable memorabilia.

While collectibles tied to major events can sometimes command huge prices, items like worn contact lenses aren’t considered legitimate memorabilia and are often restricted from resale due to hygiene and safety concerns. The moment sparked debate online about how far fandom can go and what truly holds value in modern fan culture.

title: Video by lawsofattraction2026
description:
In 1994, Jim Carrey was so broke he was sleeping in his car.

Auditions kept rejecting him.
Bills kept piling up.
Hope was running thin.

Most people would’ve quit.

Instead, Jim did something strange.

Every night, he sat alone and pictured a different future.
Not desperately.
Not angrily.
Calmly.

He imagined himself acting in major films.
He imagined financial freedom.
He imagined success so clearly it felt real.

One day, with almost no money to his name, he wrote himself a check for $10 million and dated it five years into the future.

People laughed at that part.

But what they didn’t see was the shift happening inside him.

He stopped chasing.
He started aligning.

Years later, right on time, he received $10 million for his role in “Dumb and Dumber.”

That wasn’t luck.
That was a trained mind.

I think about this whenever I hear stories of people stuck in survival mode — like a cash-on-delivery rider delivering packages all day, touching money that never stays with him, believing wealth is only for others.

Until he changes one thing.

His focus.

A few quiet minutes each morning.
No phone.
No noise.

Just intention, gratitude, and belief.

Not to force money…
but to become the person who attracts it.

Because money doesn’t respond to struggle.
It responds to clarity.

If you’re working hard but feel stuck, maybe your effort isn’t the problem.

Maybe your mindset is asking for an upgrade.

If you’re curious about manifestation and abundance consciousness…

title: Video by stelle.world
description:
The phrase highest form of intelligence isn't IQ sets the tone for a thought-provoking discussion. According to neuroscientists, this intelligence is something entirely different, and it's rare to find people who possess it. The conversation revolves around metacognition, the ability to think about one's own thinking.

This video delves into the concept of metacognition, explaining that it's the ability to observe one's mind in real time, noticing thoughts, questioning reactions, and interrupting emotional reflexes. The conversation highlights the importance of updating beliefs instead of defending them, and how this process can lead to changes in the brain. The anterior prefrontal cortex, a region responsible for self-observation, activates when one observes their own thoughts. The video also displays on-screen text, highest update beliefs interrupting emotions that can edit its own, which reinforces the idea that metacognition allows for a deeper understanding of one's thoughts and emotions. By exploring this concept, the video provides insight into the workings of the human brain and the potential for personal growth.

The idea that metacognition can be developed and strengthened over time raises interesting questions about personal development and intelligence. As the conversation suggests, quietly outgrowing others around them, those who develop metacognition can experience significant personal growth. What does it mean to truly think about one's own thinking, and how can this ability be cultivated in daily life? The video invites viewers to consider the role of metacognition in their own lives, and to reflect on the potential benefits of developing this rare form of intelligence.

#Metacognition #psychology #explore

Follow @stelle.world for tech, science and everything in between. Even intelligence, in some form this is true, but in some form this is dubious.

title: Post by soulmindhub
description:
Neuroscience distinguishes raw cognitive ability from metacognition—the capacity to monitor, evaluate, and regulate one’s own thoughts and emotions. This self-reflective process engages higher-order prefrontal networks involved in monitoring and control, allowing individuals to interrupt automatic reactions and update beliefs based on new information.

Functional imaging studies show that observing and labeling internal states (“I’m anxious,” “I’m angry”) increases activity in regulatory prefrontal regions while reducing reactivity in limbic areas such as the amygdala. This shift supports emotional regulation, reduced impulsivity, and improved decision-making.

Memory research further indicates that when experiences are recalled with conscious awareness, they enter a reconsolidation window—during which emotional intensity can diminish and interpretations can be modified. Over time, repeated self-observation and labeling reshape neural signaling patterns through plasticity.

Metacognition is not motivational rhetoric. It reflects measurable changes in brain activity associated with learning, emotional control, and adaptive behavior. Growth occurs not by suppressing thoughts, but by noticing them—creating the distance needed for the brain to update itself.

✅ FOLLOW @soulmindhub for more

title: Video by pagetkagy
description:
Science proved THIS changes reality ⬇️
ㅤ
In the Double Slit Experiment, particles don’t behave like solid matter until they’re observed. Before observation, they exist as probabilities. The act of observing collapses those probabilities into a single outcome. This isn’t philosophy - it’s measured PHYSICS.
ㅤ
Your brain works the same way. Your subconscious attention acts as the observer, constantly collapsing one version of reality out of many possible ones. When your focus is trained on threat, lack, or resistance, that’s the timeline that keeps rendering.
ㅤ
The Delayed Choice Experiment went even further, showing that observation can influence outcomes backward in time. Which explains why, when your internal state shifts, past events suddenly make sense differently and opportunities appear where nothing existed before.
ㅤ
You’re not manifesting incorrectly. You’re observing consistently. Change the observer, and the reality being rendered has no choice but to change with it.
ㅤ
Follow @pagetkagy to train the observer that shapes your reality ✨
ㅤ
#quantumphysics #observereffect #doubleslitexperiment #timelineshift #selfconcept #identityshift #scienceandmind

title: Post by the.alchemist
description:
Metacognition is one of those words that sounds clinical until you realize what it actually means. It's the ability to observe your own mind while it's operating, to watch a thought arise and recognize it as a thought rather than reality.

Researchers at University College London found that people with higher metacognitive ability have more gray matter in the anterior prefrontal cortex, the part of the brain responsible for self-reflection and decision-making.

 This isn't just a personality trait you're born with or without. Studies show metacognition can be developed through deliberate practice, meaning the witness that's been inside you this whole time can be strengthened like a muscle. 

Every wisdom tradition has pointed to this capacity in its own language, but now we have the science to back it up. The question is whether you'll keep living inside your thoughts or finally step back and meet the part of you that's been watching all along.

#metacognition #neuroscience #selfawareness

title: Post by soulmindhub
description:
Otto Warburg (1883–1970) - A Nobel Prize winning biochemist who reshaped how science understands cellular energy.

In 1931, Warburg received the Nobel Prize for uncovering how cells generate energy — pioneering the field of cellular metabolism. His work later described what became known as the Warburg Effect: the observation that many cancer cells rely heavily on glucose fermentation rather than efficient oxygen-based respiration.

Warburg was not a fringe thinker. He was one of the most influential scientists of the 20th century, nominated for the Nobel Prize dozens of times and deeply respected by his peers. His ideas sparked decades of research into metabolism, mitochondria, and disease — debates that continue in modern science today.

His legacy lives on wherever researchers ask a fundamental question: What happens when the way a cell makes energy goes wrong?

Science evolves. Questions remain. Curiosity drives progress.

✅ FOLLOW @soulmindhub for more

title: Post by theneurofactory
description:
She was nominated 7 times for the Nobel Prize. She never received it.

Dr. Johanna Budwig was a German biochemist with doctorates in physics and chemistry. She worked as Chief Expert for pharmaceuticals and fats at the German Federal Health Office.

In the 1950s, she discovered something that threatened everything the food and pharmaceutical industries were built on.

She found that people with cancer were consistently deficient in polyunsaturated fatty acids. Without these fats, cell membranes break down. Oxygen can't enter cells. And when cells lose oxygen, cancer thrives.

Her solution was almost too simple. Flaxseed oil mixed with cottage cheese. The sulfhydryl proteins in cottage cheese bond with omega-3s in flaxseed oil, making them absorbable by cells. This floods cells with oxygen.

She reported extraordinary results across multiple cancer types. Studies on flaxseed have since confirmed its ability to reduce tumor growth, cut metastasis by up to 70%, and enhance the effects of existing cancer treatments.

So why haven't you heard of her?

Because she refused to sell her protocol to pharmaceutical companies who wanted to patent it and profit from it. She openly attacked trans fats, processed oils, and margarine decades before the mainstream caught on.

She was blackballed. Silenced. Erased from medical history.

She died in 2003 at the age of 94. Her work lives on through those who share it.

Save this. Share it with someone who needs to see it.

title: Video by soulfulsongs77
description:
The way he stopped playing guitar just to hear them sing was priceless✨

“More Than Words” by Extreme was released in 1990 as the third single from their second studio album Pornograffitti. The song became the band’s biggest commercial success, reaching top hit and gaining worldwide popularity for its stripped-down acoustic style, which contrasted sharply with the band’s usual hard rock sound. 

Musically, the track is built around gentle acoustic guitar fingerpicking by Nuno Bettencourt and soft, intimate vocals from Gary Cherone, creating a minimalist arrangement that places emotional emphasis on the message. 

Lyrically, the song explores the idea that saying “I love you” is not enough without meaningful actions to support those words; it challenges superficial expressions of love and calls for sincerity and commitment. 

Its heartfelt delivery and simplicity helped it become one of the most recognizable acoustic ballads of the early 1990s

dm for credits or removal

title: Video by sheinspiration___
description:
Most parents would NEVER knowingly give their kids something that harms them…

But what if the things we were taught are “normal”…
are actually the problem?

The same system that normalized sugary breakfast cereals for kids
was built on marketing, not health.

Bright boxes. Cartoon characters. “Part of a balanced breakfast.”

Meanwhile…
we’re seeing more kids struggle with energy crashes, focus issues, weight gain, and blood sugar problems earlier than ever.

This isn’t about guilt — it’s about awareness.

Because once you SEE it, you can’t unsee it.

I had to unlearn a lot too.
And when I did, everything changed — not just for me, but for my kids and my whole family.

We didn’t need perfection…
we just needed a better approach to protect ourselves from what’s out there.

Curious what you think about this 👇
Are cereals something you still keep in your home?

If you want to learn the natural approach we use daily to support our health in today’s food environment, comment PROTOCOL and I’ll reach out 💬

title: Post by theneurofactory
description:
A $2 veterinary pill is doing what $10,000/month cancer drugs can't.

It's called fenbendazole.

It was designed to deworm animals. But researchers discovered it kills cancer cells through multiple mechanisms — starving them of glucose, stopping them from dividing, and forcing them to self-destruct.

In 2016, Joe Tippens was given 3 months to live with terminal lung cancer. He started taking fenbendazole alongside vitamin E, CBD oil, and curcumin. Three months later, his PET scan came back clean. Zero cancer cells detected.

He was the only one cured out of 1,100 patients in his clinical trial.

Since then, lab studies have shown fenbendazole works against lung, breast, colorectal, prostate, cervical, and pancreatic cancer cells.

A 2025 case series documented three Stage IV cancer patients achieving complete or near-complete remission after self-administering fenbendazole — without chemotherapy.

So why haven't you heard about this?

Because the patent expired in the 1990s. No pharma company will spend billions running trials on a drug they can't own. There's no ROI. So the research stays small, underfunded, and ignored.

Meanwhile, cancer drugs that cost families their life savings get fast-tracked through approval.

This post isn't medical advice. It's information that should be public. The science exists. The case reports exist. The mechanisms are well understood.

What's missing is funding. Ask yourself why.

Save this. Send it to someone who needs it.

💡 Comment "GUIDE" and I'll send you our free newsletter where 20,000+ people get suppressed research and health protocols the system doesn't want you knowing.

P.S. Real health starts with real food. The Ancestral Kitchen Bible has 100+ nutrient-dense recipes built on ancestral wisdom Big Food buried. Click the link in bio to get it now (Use code "2026" for 50% OFF)

title: Video by williamjsper
description:
1. In 1924, researcher Elton Mayo conducted an experiment that many later tried to bury. He told workers they were being “observed for productivity.” And it was true — they were constantly monitored. Yet within weeks, they began to improve: more focus, higher output, greater initiative. A simple observation changed real behavior — as if the brain had received a “silent command.”

2. Years later, other researchers repeated the study with self-observation. One group was told they possessed an “internal monitoring ability.” The tasks were identical, but those who observed their own thoughts produced responses that were 94% more accurate. The scientists were clear: “We didn’t increase ability. We changed the way they observed themselves.”

3. One participant summarized it this way: “I just watched my thoughts… and then I controlled them.” Mayo explained that when the mind directs conscious attention toward itself, the body begins to act as if under direct command. The brain doesn’t respond to talent — it responds to self-observation.

4. The dark side is the opposite: when someone ignores their own thoughts and lives on autopilot, the brain acts chaotically. Lack of self-observation reduces mental control almost as much as chronic fatigue. The body operates without direction, aligning with the internal void that’s been created.

5. A Harvard psychologist put it plainly: “We become aware of who we are when we watch our thoughts — until we realize we never did.” By changing internal self-observation, the nervous system reorganizes. This is the moment you stop living on autopilot and start living consciously.

title: Video by cristophergeilermd
description:
🧬Your body already makes its own longevity molecule. After 40, you’re running out of it. 🧬Alpha-ketoglutarate (AKG) is a metabolite your mitochondria produce as part of the Krebs cycle. It’s not a drug. It’s not synthetic. But its mechanism of action on aging biology is extraordinary.Here’s what it does simultaneously:
🔴 Inhibits mTOR - the chronic activation of this growth-signaling pathway is one of the primary drivers of cellular aging. AKG puts the brakes on it.
🔵 Rewrites your epigenome - AKG is a required cofactor for TET demethylases and histone demethylases (KDMs), the enzymes that regulate which genes get silenced as you age. It directly modulates H3K9me3 and H3K27me3 marks.
🟢 Mimics caloric restriction - AKG levels naturally rise with fasting, exercise, and dietary restriction. In a landmark 2020 study in Cell Metabolism, aging mice given supplemental calcium-AKG lived longer, showed significantly less frailty, had lower systemic inflammatory cytokines, and demonstrated what the authors called a compression of morbidity - more life, but critically, healthier life. 🐭. The ABLE Trial - a double-blind, placebo-controlled RCT currently underway - is now testing 1g of sustained-release Ca-AKG versus placebo in 120 adults aged 40-60, with DNA methylation age as the primary endpoint. 📚 References:Asadi Shahmirzadi A, et al. Alpha-Ketoglutarate, an Endogenous Metabolite, Extends Lifespan and Compresses Morbidity in Aging Mice. Cell Metabolism. 2020;32(3):447-456. doi:10.1016/j.cmet.2020.08.004Chin RM, et al. The metabolite α-ketoglutarate extends lifespan by inhibiting ATP synthase and TOR. Nature. 2014;510:397-401. doi:10.1038/nature13264
Sandalova E, et al. Alpha-ketoglutarate supplementation and BiologicaL agE in middle-aged adults (ABLE) - intervention study protocol. GeroScience. 2023;45:2897-2907. doi:10.1007/s11357-023 Bayliak MM, Lushchak VI. Mimicking caloric restriction for anti-aging effects: The pro-oxidant role of alpha-ketoglutarate. Advances in Protein Chemistry and Structural Biology. 2022. #AlphaKetoglutarate #AKG #CaAKG #LongevityScience #LongevityMedicine
